uses: Consolida segetum, also known as field larkspur, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant contains alkaloids, including delphinine and ajacine, which have been reported to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. In traditional medicine, Consolida segetum has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily based on its traditional use and limited scientific studies.

intro: Consolida segetum, also known as cornflower, is a small annual herbaceous plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The health benefits of Consolida segetum include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, particularly isopycnenine, isopelletierine, and consolaidine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Consolida segetum is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to soothe respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
